metadata.homeolog_convention: DELLA split (Rht-B1, Rht-D1 separate); GA-pathway and BR-receptor genes composite (TaGA20ox, TaGA3ox, TaGA2ox, TaGID1, TaBRI1, TaBZR1, TaBIN2, VRN1, TaGRF4, TaARF, TaIAA, TaTIR1); allele-specific where phenotype is driven by named allele (PLATZ-A1, TaARF4, TaERF-A1, Ppd-D1, TaOSCA1.4, Rht18, Rht24, Rht12).
metadata.naming_notes: Canonical names (Rht-B1, TaGID1, Ppd-D1, TaERF-A1 etc) are normalised to ALL_CAPS with underscores (RHT_B1, TAGID1, PPD_D1, TAERF_A1) to satisfy the GENE naming regex. Lowercase enzyme suffixes such as 'ox' in TaGA2ox → TAGA2OX. Aliases preserved in description / node_annotations.json.
residual_gaps_handled: G001 (TaCPS/TaKS/TaKO/TaKAO early GA biosynthesis): documented in curated_edges.json only; no perturbation tests map to these enzymes, so excluding them from network.json keeps Gibberellin activator count within the 7-cap without losing test coverage (literature_gap).
G005 (Rht8 upstream): no published regulator in wheat — retained as source, matches biology.
G006 (TaTB1): review-level only — OMITTED from network.json (literature_gap) rather than adding with weak edges.
G007 (Auxin→TaGA20ox crosstalk): no OA wheat primary paper — OMITTED (literature_gap); would bridge auxin to GA in future iterations.
G009 partial (TaGA20ox RNAi, Uniconazole primary): paywalled — perturbation coverage limited but TaGA20ox node included so mapping is possible.
TaGRF1 sink-only: OMITTED (alternative (b) from judge report).
Rht4/5/26 uncloned: OMITTED at network level (documented in curated_edges literature_gap_log).

nodes.description: Long cold exposure required to activate VRN1 and promote spring growth.
Light-cycle input that entrains Ppd-1 and coordinates flowering and stem elongation.
Phytochrome-sensed red/far-red light; targets PIF transcription factors for degradation.
Nitrate signal that upregulates GA biosynthesis and destabilises DELLA proteins.
Bioactive gibberellin pool; balance of TaGA20ox/TaGA3ox biosynthesis vs TaGA2ox catabolism.
Active brassinosteroid pool produced by TaD11 and rate-limiting TaDWF4.
Active auxin pool; conjugated (inactivated) by TaGH3.
SCF F-box component that ubiquitinates GA-bound DELLA for proteasomal degradation.
GA 3-oxidase; catalyses 3β-hydroxylation of GA20→GA1 and GA9→GA4 (bioactive step).
1β-hydroxylase that diverts GA9/GA20 to inactive GA61/GA60.
BR co-receptor; heterodimerises with TaBRI1 upon BR binding.
BR signalling activator; ubiquitinates TaBKI1 for proteasomal destruction.
Homeolog-specific ARF4 associated with plant height and root QTLs.
AP2/ERF TF that directly activates TaPIF4 transcription; JD6 semi-dwarf allele (F178S).
Green-Revolution replacement semi-dwarfing locus; modifies GA biosynthesis; GA-responsive.
Rht13 (Rht-B13b S240F) is an autoactive NB-LRR that triggers PR-gene upregulation.
Semi-dwarf allele that upregulates TaGA2ox-A9 (~4-fold), lowering bioactive GA.
Ancient dwarfing allele that elevates TaGA2ox-A9 expression in stems.
Dwarfing allele whose SNP-390(C/A) elevates TaGA2oxA13 expression.
Novel Rht gene; GA-independent Ca2+-signalling route. RNAi reduces plant height 11-16%.
Late-step BR biosynthesis enzyme (C6-oxidase).
22α-hydroxylation of campesterol; rate-limiting branch-point of BR biosynthesis.
GA receptor; GA-bound TaGID1 recruits SCF-TaSLY1 to degrade DELLA.
GA 20-oxidase; rate-limiting late GA biosynthesis enzyme.

nodes.full_name: Ta GA 2-oxidase (TaGA2ox family; A9, A13, A3)
nodes.description: GA catabolism; inactivates GA1/GA4 and precursor GA12.
DELLA growth repressor on 4B; Rht-B1b/c are GA-insensitive Green Revolution alleles.
DELLA homeolog on 4D; Rht-D1b is the second Green Revolution dwarfing allele.
PLATZ TF that physically interacts with DELLA-GRAS; promotes GRF1/CYCD4 expression.
GRF TF that antagonises DELLA; OE increases yield and N/C assimilation.
bHLH TF activating cell-elongation genes; sequestered by DELLA and degraded by phytochromes.
Photoperiod-1 homeolog on 2D; dominant Ppd-D1a allele shortens internodes and reduces height.
Vernalization-induced MADS-box TF that activates TaGA20ox2.
BR receptor LRR-RLK; heterodimerises with TaSERK upon BL binding.
Plasma-membrane inhibitor of TaBRI1 in the BR-off state; degraded by TaZnF upon BR signalling.
Negative regulator of BR signalling; inactivated via BSK/CDG/BSU1 upon BR perception.
Dephosphorylated active BZR1/BES1 drives BR-response growth genes.
Auxin co-receptor F-box; auxin-loaded TaTIR1 targets TaIAA for degradation.
Aux/IAA repressors that sequester TaARF in the auxin-off state.
Auxin-response TF family; activates cell-elongation genes upon TaIAA removal.
Auxin-linked HD-Zip TF that represses TaGH3 expression.
Conjugates IAA to amino acids, reducing the active auxin pool.
Cell-wall cross-linking enzymes whose upregulation limits cell elongation.
Wheat plant height; integrated output of GA-DELLA, BR, auxin, light, and specialty-Rht pathways.

edges.mechanism: GA 20-oxidase catalyses GA12→GA9 and GA53→GA20; rate-limiting late GA biosynthesis.

evidence.title: Function and transcript analysis of gibberellin-biosynthetic enzymes in wheat
evidence.authors: Appleford NEJ, Evans DJ, Lenton JR, Gaskin P, Croker SJ, Devos KM, Phillips AL, Hedden P
evidence.evidence_sentence: GA 20-oxidase and 3-oxidase are major sites of regulation in GA biosynthesis; enzymes catalyze multi-step reactions, including conversions of GA12-GA9, and GA53-GA20

evidence.title: Molecular Characterization of Rht-1 Dwarfing Genes in Hexaploid Wheat
evidence.authors: Pearce S, Saville R, Vaughan SP, Chandler PM, Wilhelm EP, Sparks CA, Al-Kaff N, Korolev A, Boulton MI, Phillips AL, Hedden P, Nicholson P, Thomas SG
evidence.evidence_sentence: expression of the biosynthetic genes is strongest in growing tissues, with TaGA20ox1 expression located most strongly at the nodes

edges.mechanism: GA 3-oxidase catalyses 3β-hydroxylation GA20→GA1 producing bioactive GA.

evidence.title: GIBBERELLIN 3-OXIDASE genes regulate height and grain size in bread wheat
evidence.authors: Phillips AL, Huttly AK, Alarcon-Reverte R, et al
evidence.evidence_sentence: GA3OX3 catalyses the 3β-hydroxylation of GA20 to GA1

edges.mechanism: GA 2-oxidase catabolises bioactive GA1/GA4 to inactive GA8/GA34.

evidence.title: Rht18 Semidwarfism in Wheat Is Due to Increased GA 2-oxidaseA9 Expression and Reduced GA Content
evidence.authors: Ford BA, Foo E, Sharwood R, Karafiatova M, Vrana J, MacMillan C, Nichols DS, Steuernagel B, Uauy C, Dolezel J, Chandler PM, Spielmeyer W
evidence.evidence_sentence: increased expression of GA2oxA9 in Rht18 results in a reduction of both bioactive GA content and plant height

edges.mechanism: TaGA1ox1 1β-hydroxylates GA9/GA20 to inactive GA61/GA60, diverting from bioactive pool.

evidence.title: GIBBERELLIN 3-OXIDASE genes regulate height and grain size in bread wheat
evidence.authors: Phillips AL, Huttly AK, Alarcon-Reverte R, et al
evidence.evidence_sentence: ga1ox1 Mutant: Bioactive GA1/GA4 extremely high; 10.7 cm taller in 2021 field trial
evidence.claim: TaGA1ox1 diverts GA precursors reducing bioactive GA

edges.mechanism: Rht8 (TaRNHL-D1) promotes bioactive GA biosynthesis in stem.

evidence.title: Cloning and functional characterization of Rht8, a Green Revolution replacement gene in wheat
evidence.authors: Xiong H, Zhou C, Fu M, Guo H, Xie Y, Zhao L, Gu J, Zhao S, Ding Y, Li Y, Liu L
evidence.evidence_sentence: controls the plant height and spike length via GA biosynthesis

edges.mechanism: Nitrate signalling activates GA metabolism gene expression, raising bioactive GA.

evidence.title: Nitrate signaling promotes plant growth by upregulating gibberellin biosynthesis and destabilization of DELLA proteins
evidence.authors: Camut L, Gallova B, Jilli L, Sirlin-Josserand M, Carrera E, Sakvarelidze-Achard L, Zumsteg J, Heintz D, Leonhardt N, Lange MJP, Lange T, Davière JM, Achard P
evidence.evidence_sentence: nitrate reduces the abundance of DELLAs by increasing GA contents through activation of GA metabolism gene expression

edges.mechanism: GA induces its own catabolism — classic GA homeostasis (GA→TaGA2ox↑→GA↓).

evidence.title: Wheat gibberellin oxidase genes and their functions in regulating tillering
evidence.authors: Wang T, Li J, Jiang Y, Zhang J, Ni Y, Zhang P, Yao Z, Jiao Z, Li H, Li L, Niu Y, Li Q, Yin G, Niu J
evidence.evidence_sentence: exogenous GA3 increases the expressions of GA2oxs

edges.mechanism: VRN1 binds and activates the TaGA20ox2 promoter, coupling vernalization to GA biosynthesis.

evidence.title: VRN1 regulates heading and plant height in wheat by activating gibberellin biosynthesis
evidence.authors: Liu T, Li D, Xie Z, et al
evidence.evidence_sentence: VRN1 binds to and activates the TaGA20ox2 promoter

edges.mechanism: DELLA promotes TaGA20ox transcription (negative-feedback GA homeostasis: DELLA↑→GA↑→DELLA↓).

evidence.title: Wheat gibberellin oxidase genes and their functions in regulating tillering
evidence.authors: Wang T, Li J, Jiang Y, Zhang J, Ni Y, Zhang P, Yao Z, Jiao Z, Li H, Li L, Niu Y, Li Q, Yin G, Niu J
evidence.evidence_sentence: DELLA protein signaling alters GA oxidase gene transcription through GA-GID1-DELLA tricomplex

edges.mechanism: Rht18 allele drives ~4-fold upregulation of TaGA2ox-A9, increasing GA catabolism.

evidence.title: Rht18 Semidwarfism in Wheat Is Due to Increased GA 2-oxidaseA9 Expression and Reduced GA Content
evidence.authors: Ford BA, Foo E, Sharwood R, Karafiatova M, Vrana J, MacMillan C, Nichols DS, Steuernagel B, Uauy C, Dolezel J, Chandler PM, Spielmeyer W
evidence.evidence_sentence: Expression of GA2oxA9 was ~4-fold higher in the Icaro and the M24 overgrowth mutant compared to the tall parent Anhinga

edges.mechanism: Rht24b cis-regulatory variant elevates TaGA2ox-A9 expression in stems.

evidence.title: Rht24b, an ancient variation of TaGA2ox-A9, reduces plant height without yield penalty in wheat
evidence.authors: Tian X, Xia X, Xu D, Liu Y, Xie L, Hassan MA, Song J, Li F, Wang D, Zhang Y, Hao Y, Li G, Chu C, He Z, Cao S
evidence.evidence_sentence: Rht24b conferred higher expression of TaGA2ox-A9 in stems, leading to a reduction of bioactive GA in stems

edges.mechanism: Rht12b SNP-390(C/A) elevates TaGA2oxA13 expression; causal variant of the Rht12 locus.

evidence.title: Rht12b, a widely used ancient allele of TaGA2oxA13, reduces plant height and enhances yield potential in wheat
evidence.authors: Zhang H, Xu W, Wang H, Hu L, Li Y, Qi X, Zhang L, Li C, Hua X
evidence.evidence_sentence: Rht12b conferred higher expression level of TaGA2oxA13, and transient activation assays defined SNP-390(C/A) in the promoter of TaGA2oxA13 as the causal variation
evidence.claim: Rht12b promoter allele upregulates TaGA2oxA13 transcription

evidence.title: Overgrowth mutants determine the causal role of gibberellin GA2oxidaseA13 in Rht12 dwarfism of wheat
evidence.authors: Buss W, Ford BA, Foo E, Spielmeyer W
evidence.evidence_sentence: tall overgrowth individuals contain loss-of-function mutations in GA2oxidaseA13

edges.mechanism: GA binding in the TaGID1 pocket induces conformational change enabling DELLA interaction.

evidence.title: Molecular Characterization of Rht-1 Dwarfing Genes in Hexaploid Wheat
evidence.authors: Pearce S, Saville R, Vaughan SP, Chandler PM, Wilhelm EP, Sparks CA, Al-Kaff N, Korolev A, Boulton MI, Phillips AL, Hedden P, Nicholson P, Thomas SG
evidence.evidence_sentence: Binding of bioactive GAs within a pocket in the GID1 protein induces a conformational change that permits interaction between the DELLA and GID1 proteins
evidence.claim: GA binding activates TaGID1 to interact with DELLA

evidence.title: Molecular characterization of three GIBBERELLIN-INSENSITIVE DWARF1 homologous genes in hexaploid wheat
evidence.authors: Li A, Yang W, Li S, Liu D, Guo X, Sun J, Zhang A
evidence.evidence_sentence: Strong interactions between each putative TaGID1 and the wheat DELLA proteins RHT-A1, RHT-B1, and RHT-D1 in the presence of GA3, and weak interactions in the absence of GA3
evidence.claim: GA3 strengthens TaGID1-DELLA binding in Y2H

edges.mechanism: GA-bound TaGID1 binds Rht-B1 DELLA N-terminal motif, recruiting SCF-SLY1 for ubiquitination.

evidence.title: Molecular Characterization of Rht-1 Dwarfing Genes in Hexaploid Wheat
evidence.authors: Pearce S, Saville R, Vaughan SP, Chandler PM, Wilhelm EP, Sparks CA, Al-Kaff N, Korolev A, Boulton MI, Phillips AL, Hedden P, Nicholson P, Thomas SG
evidence.evidence_sentence: This leads to closing of the N-terminal lid of GID1, allowing DELLAs to bind through their conserved N-terminal DELLA/TVHYNP motifs
evidence.claim: GA-TaGID1 targets Rht-B1 DELLA for degradation

edges.mechanism: GA-bound TaGID1 binds Rht-D1 DELLA homeolog for SCF-SLY1-mediated degradation.

evidence.title: Molecular characterization of three GIBBERELLIN-INSENSITIVE DWARF1 homologous genes in hexaploid wheat
evidence.authors: Li A, Yang W, Li S, Liu D, Guo X, Sun J, Zhang A
evidence.evidence_sentence: Strong interactions between each putative TaGID1 and the wheat DELLA proteins RHT-A1, RHT-B1, and RHT-D1 in the presence of GA3
evidence.claim: TaGID1 degrades Rht-D1 DELLA homeolog in presence of GA3

edges.mechanism: SCF-TaSLY1 F-box ubiquitinates Rht-B1 DELLA for 26S proteasomal degradation.

evidence.title: Molecular Characterization of Rht-1 Dwarfing Genes in Hexaploid Wheat
evidence.authors: Pearce S, Saville R, Vaughan SP, Chandler PM, Wilhelm EP, Sparks CA, Al-Kaff N, Korolev A, Boulton MI, Phillips AL, Hedden P, Nicholson P, Thomas SG
evidence.evidence_sentence: GID1/DELLA binding enables recognition by the GID2/SLEEPY1 F-box component of an SCF Ub E3 ligase, leading to ubiquitination of DELLAs and their subsequent 26S proteasome-mediated degradation

edges.mechanism: SCF-TaSLY1 ubiquitinates Rht-D1 DELLA homeolog (parallel to Rht-B1).

evidence.title: Molecular Characterization of Rht-1 Dwarfing Genes in Hexaploid Wheat
evidence.authors: Pearce S, Saville R, Vaughan SP, Chandler PM, Wilhelm EP, Sparks CA, Al-Kaff N, Korolev A, Boulton MI, Phillips AL, Hedden P, Nicholson P, Thomas SG
evidence.evidence_sentence: GID1/DELLA binding enables recognition by the GID2/SLEEPY1 F-box component of an SCF Ub E3 ligase, leading to ubiquitination of DELLAs

edges.mechanism: Nitrate promotes GA-mediated destabilisation of DELLA (partial rescue by global-della mutant).

evidence.title: Nitrate signaling promotes plant growth by upregulating gibberellin biosynthesis and destabilization of DELLA proteins
evidence.authors: Camut L, Gallova B, Jilli L, Sirlin-Josserand M, Carrera E, Sakvarelidze-Achard L, Zumsteg J, Heintz D, Leonhardt N, Lange MJP, Lange T, Davière JM, Achard P
evidence.evidence_sentence: the growth restraint conferred by nitrate deficiency is partially rescued in global-DELLA mutant

edges.mechanism: Rht-B1 DELLA represses GA-responsive growth; Rht-B1b reduces height ~20%, Rht-B1c ~50%.

evidence.title: Molecular Characterization of Rht-1 Dwarfing Genes in Hexaploid Wheat
evidence.authors: Pearce S, Saville R, Vaughan SP, Chandler PM, Wilhelm EP, Sparks CA, Al-Kaff N, Korolev A, Boulton MI, Phillips AL, Hedden P, Nicholson P, Thomas SG
evidence.evidence_sentence: DELLA proteins, which act to repress GA-responsive growth, and their mutant alleles Rht-B1b and Rht-D1b are thought to confer dwarfism by producing more active forms of these growth repressors

evidence.title: The Impact of the Wheat Rht-B1b Semi-Dwarfing Allele on Photosynthesis and Seed Development Under Field Conditions
evidence.authors: Jobson EM, Johnston RE, Oiestad AJ, Martin JM, Giroux MJ
evidence.evidence_sentence: Plant height (cm) 95.0 (Rht-B1a) vs 73.1 (Rht-B1b), -23% reduction significant

edges.mechanism: Rht-D1 DELLA homeolog represses growth; Rht-D1b Green-Revolution dwarfing allele ~20%.

evidence.title: Molecular Characterization of Rht-1 Dwarfing Genes in Hexaploid Wheat
evidence.authors: Pearce S, Saville R, Vaughan SP, Chandler PM, Wilhelm EP, Sparks CA, Al-Kaff N, Korolev A, Boulton MI, Phillips AL, Hedden P, Nicholson P, Thomas SG
evidence.evidence_sentence: Rht-B1 and Rht-D1 encode DELLA proteins, which act to repress GA-responsive growth

edges.mechanism: DELLA GRAS PFYRE subdomain physically binds and sequesters PLATZ-A1 (Y2H + genetics).

evidence.title: Wheat plant height locus RHT25 encodes a PLATZ transcription factor that interacts with DELLA (RHT1)
evidence.authors: Zhang Z, Belcram H, Gornicki P, Charles M, Just J, Huneau C, Magdelenat G, Couloux A, Samain S, Gill BS, Rasmussen JB, Barbe V, Faris JD, Chalhoub B
evidence.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences USA
evidence.evidence_sentence: PLATZ-A1 showed a positive interaction with RHT1-GRAS in yeast-two-hybrid assays

edges.mechanism: DELLA binds and inhibits TaGRF4 growth output; antagonistic balance (Li et al 2018).

evidence.title: Modulating plant growth-metabolism coordination for sustainable agriculture
evidence.authors: Li S, Tian Y, Wu K, Ye Y, Yu J, Zhang J, Liu Q, Hu M, Li H, Tong Y, Harberd NP, Fu X
evidence.evidence_sentence: physical interactions of the GROWTH-REGULATING FACTOR 4 (GRF4) transcription factor and the growth inhibitor DELLA confer homeostatic co-regulation

edges.mechanism: TaGRF4 competes with PLATZ1 for DELLA binding and antagonises DELLA repression.

evidence.title: Modulating plant growth-metabolism coordination for sustainable agriculture
evidence.authors: Li S, Tian Y, Wu K, Ye Y, Yu J, Zhang J, Liu Q, Hu M, Li H, Tong Y, Harberd NP, Fu X
evidence.evidence_sentence: The balanced opposing activities and physical interactions of the GROWTH-REGULATING FACTOR 4 (GRF4) transcription factor and the growth inhibitor DELLA confer homeostatic co-regulation of growth

evidence.title: Wheat plant height locus RHT25 encodes a PLATZ transcription factor that interacts with DELLA (RHT1)
evidence.authors: Zhang Z, Belcram H, Gornicki P, Charles M, Just J, Huneau C, Magdelenat G, Couloux A, Samain S, Gill BS, Rasmussen JB, Barbe V, Faris JD, Chalhoub B
evidence.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences USA
evidence.evidence_sentence: expression of GRF4 as the third protein reduced the interaction between PLATZ1 and DELLA by 74%
evidence.claim: GRF4 competes with PLATZ1 for DELLA binding

edges.mechanism: PLATZ-A1 (Rht25) LOF reduces height ~12.7%; OE increases height (causal Rht25 gene).

evidence.title: Wheat plant height locus RHT25 encodes a PLATZ transcription factor that interacts with DELLA (RHT1)
evidence.authors: Zhang Z, Belcram H, Gornicki P, Charles M, Just J, Huneau C, Magdelenat G, Couloux A, Samain S, Gill BS, Rasmussen JB, Barbe V, Faris JD, Chalhoub B
evidence.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences USA
evidence.evidence_sentence: Natural and induced loss-of-function mutations in PLATZ-A1 reduce plant height and its overexpression increases plant height, demonstrating that PLATZ-A1 is the causative gene of RHT25

edges.mechanism: DELLA sequesters PIF TFs; accumulated Rht-B1c constitutively blocks PIF activity.

evidence.title: Sequence variations of the partially dominant DELLA gene Rht-B1c in wheat and their functional impacts
evidence.authors: Wen W, Deng Q, Jia H, Wei L, Wei J, Wan H, Yang L, Cao W, Ma Z
evidence.evidence_sentence: The accumulated mutant protein constitutively blocks phytochrome-interacting factor activity, suppressing cell elongation genes

edges.mechanism: PIFs activate cell-elongation genes; DELLA-sequestration decreases stem elongation.

evidence.title: Sequence variations of the partially dominant DELLA gene Rht-B1c in wheat and their functional impacts
evidence.authors: Wen W, Deng Q, Jia H, Wei L, Wei J, Wan H, Yang L, Cao W, Ma Z
evidence.evidence_sentence: constitutively blocks phytochrome-interacting factor activity, suppressing cell elongation genes

edges.mechanism: Red/far-red light via phytochromes targets PIFs for degradation.

evidence.title: Sequence variations of the partially dominant DELLA gene Rht-B1c in wheat and their functional impacts
evidence.authors: Wen W, Deng Q, Jia H, Wei L, Wei J, Wan H, Yang L, Cao W, Ma Z
evidence.evidence_sentence: phytochrome-interacting factor activity, suppressing cell elongation genes

edges.mechanism: TaERF-A1 directly binds the TaPIF4 promoter and activates its transcription (EMSA/ChIP).

evidence.title: Natural variation in TaERF-A1 confers semi-dwarf and lodging-resistant plant architecture in wheat
evidence.authors: Li L, Sheng X, Zhang H, Chen J, Zhu R, Wang C, Ren Y, Lin Y, Chen D, Lv L, Zhang Q, Wang Y, Wang H
evidence.evidence_sentence: TaERF-A1 directly associates with the promoter of TaPIF4 to activate its expression

edges.mechanism: TaD11 (C6-oxidase) catalyses late BR biosynthesis step; tad11-2a reduces endogenous BR.

evidence.title: The brassinosteroid biosynthesis gene TaD11-2A controls grain size and its elite haplotype improves wheat grain yields
evidence.authors: Xu H, Sun H, Dong J, et al
evidence.evidence_sentence: The tad11-2a-1 mutant displayed dwarfism, smaller grains, sensitivity to 24-epiBL, and reduced endogenous BR contents

edges.mechanism: TaDWF4 (CYP90B) 22α-hydroxylation of campesterol — rate-limiting BR biosynthesis branch-point.

evidence.title: Over-expression of TaDWF4 increases wheat productivity under low and sufficient nitrogen through enhanced carbon assimilation
evidence.authors: Milner MJ, Swarbreck SM, Craze M, Bowden S, Griffiths H, Bentley AR, Wallington EJ
evidence.evidence_sentence: Manipulating levels of DWF4/CYP90B, the rate-limiting step in the BR synthesis pathway, has been shown to increase yield
evidence.claim: TaDWF4 catalyses the rate-limiting step of BR biosynthesis

edges.mechanism: BL ligand binds TaBRI1 extracellular domain, activating receptor kinase and downstream cascade.

evidence.title: Induced variation in BRASSINOSTEROID INSENSITIVE 1 (BRI1) confers a compact wheat architecture
evidence.authors: Gill MS, Phillips AL, Tarkowska D, et al
evidence.evidence_sentence: BR hormones regulate critical developmental traits in wheat through the BRI1 receptor

edges.mechanism: TaSERK (BAK1 co-receptor) heterodimerises with TaBRI1, required for BR perception.

evidence.title: Wheat Brassinosteroid-Insensitive1 (TaBRI1) Interacts with Members of TaSERK Gene Family and Cause Early Flowering and Seed Yield Enhancement in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Singh A, Breja P, Khurana JP, Khurana P
evidence.evidence_sentence: TaBRI1 heterodimerizes with the TaSERKs viz. TaSERK1, TaSERK2, TaSERK3, TaSERK4 and TaSERK5

edges.mechanism: TaBKI1 represses TaBRI1 kinase activity in the BR-off state.

evidence.title: Reducing brassinosteroid signalling enhances grain yield in semi-dwarf wheat
evidence.authors: Song L, Liu J, Cao B, Liu B, Zhang X, Chen Z, Dong C, Liu X, Zhang Z, Wang W, Chai L, Liu J, Zhu J, Cui S, He F, Peng H, Hu Z, Su Z, Guo W, Xin M, Yao Y, Yan Y, Song Y, Bai G, Sun Q, Ni Z
evidence.evidence_sentence: BR signalling repressor BRI1 kinase inhibitor 1 (TaBKI1)

edges.mechanism: Activated TaBRI1 facilitates TaZnF-mediated ubiquitination of TaBKI1 (mutual release loop).

evidence.title: Reducing brassinosteroid signalling enhances grain yield in semi-dwarf wheat
evidence.authors: Song L, Liu J, Cao B, Liu B, Zhang X, Chen Z, Dong C, Liu X, Zhang Z, Wang W, Chai L, Liu J, Zhu J, Cui S, He F, Peng H, Hu Z, Su Z, Guo W, Xin M, Yao Y, Yan Y, Song Y, Bai G, Sun Q, Ni Z
evidence.evidence_sentence: TaBRI1 facilitates the ZnF-TaBKI1 conjugation in response to BR signalling

edges.mechanism: TaZnF RING E3 ligase ubiquitinates TaBKI1 for proteasomal destruction.

evidence.title: Reducing brassinosteroid signalling enhances grain yield in semi-dwarf wheat
evidence.authors: Song L, Liu J, Cao B, Liu B, Zhang X, Chen Z, Dong C, Liu X, Zhang Z, Wang W, Chai L, Liu J, Zhu J, Cui S, He F, Peng H, Hu Z, Su Z, Guo W, Xin M, Yao Y, Yan Y, Song Y, Bai G, Sun Q, Ni Z
evidence.evidence_sentence: ZnF acts as a BR signalling activator to facilitate proteasomal destruction of the BR signalling repressor BRI1 kinase inhibitor 1 (TaBKI1)

edges.mechanism: BRI1-BSK-CDG-BSU1 cascade dephosphorylates and inactivates TaBIN2 (GSK3-like kinase).

evidence.title: Induced variation in BRASSINOSTEROID INSENSITIVE 1 (BRI1) confers a compact wheat architecture
evidence.authors: Gill MS, Phillips AL, Tarkowska D, et al
evidence.evidence_sentence: In BR signaling, transphosphorylation events mediated through BSK1, CDG1 and BIN2 kinases lead to the activation of BSU1 phosphatase, which acts to dephosphorylate and inactivate BIN2, the negative regulator of BR signaling

edges.mechanism: TaBIN2 phosphorylates TaBZR1/TaBES1, blocking nuclear localisation and DNA binding.

evidence.title: Induced variation in BRASSINOSTEROID INSENSITIVE 1 (BRI1) confers a compact wheat architecture
evidence.authors: Gill MS, Phillips AL, Tarkowska D, et al
evidence.evidence_sentence: At low BR concentrations, BIN2 phosphorylates two homologous transcription factors, BZR1 and BES1/BZR2, and inhibits their nuclear localization and DNA-binding activity

edges.mechanism: Dephosphorylated TaBZR1/TaBES1 activates BR-response growth and cell-elongation genes.

evidence.title: Induced variation in BRASSINOSTEROID INSENSITIVE 1 (BRI1) confers a compact wheat architecture
evidence.authors: Gill MS, Phillips AL, Tarkowska D, et al
evidence.evidence_sentence: The dephosphorylated active transcription factors, BZR1 and BES1/BZR2, can then bind to specific sites on DNA and regulate expression of several genes

edges.mechanism: TaBZR1 physically binds DELLA proteins; reciprocal sequestration (BR-GA antagonism).

evidence.title: Induced variation in BRASSINOSTEROID INSENSITIVE 1 (BRI1) confers a compact wheat architecture
evidence.authors: Gill MS, Phillips AL, Tarkowska D, et al
evidence.evidence_sentence: BRs and GAs co-regulate a large number of common target genes and BZR1 directly interacts with DELLA proteins

edges.mechanism: TaBIN2 phosphorylates and stabilises DELLA (when BR low); BR-GA crosstalk.

evidence.title: Induced variation in BRASSINOSTEROID INSENSITIVE 1 (BRI1) confers a compact wheat architecture
evidence.authors: Gill MS, Phillips AL, Tarkowska D, et al
evidence.evidence_sentence: BRs and GAs co-regulate a large number of common target genes and BZR1 directly interacts with DELLA proteins; Deletions of domains required for BZR1-DELLA interaction abolished the antagonistic effect

edges.mechanism: Auxin binds TaTIR1/AFB F-box forming co-receptor with Aux/IAA substrate.

evidence.title: Characterization and Expression Patterns of Auxin Response Factors in Wheat
evidence.authors: Qiao L, Zhang W, Li X, Zhang L, Zhang X, Li X, Guo H, Ren Y, Zheng J, Chang Z
evidence.evidence_sentence: When present, auxin promotes the TIR1/AFB-mediated ubiquitin-proteasome-dependent degradation of inhibitory Aux/IAA proteins

evidence.title: Characterization and Expression Patterns of Auxin Response Factors in Wheat

evidence.title: TaARF4 genes are linked to root growth and plant height in wheat
evidence.authors: Wang J, Wang R, Mao X, Li L, Chang X, Zhang X, Jing R
evidence.evidence_sentence: TaARF4-A was shown to regulate HB33 expression which negatively responded to ABA, and reduced root length and plant height by repressing expression of Gretchen Hagen 3 (GH3) genes

edges.mechanism: TaHB33 represses TaGH3 expression, indirectly raising active auxin.

edges.mechanism: Ppd-1 is a pseudo-response regulator entrained by day length; long-day activates Ppd-1.

evidence.title: The Photoperiod-Insensitive Allele Ppd-D1a Promotes Earlier Flowering in Rht12 Dwarf Plants of Bread Wheat
evidence.authors: Chen Z, Cheng X, Chai L, Wang Z, Du D, Wang Z, Bian R, Zhao A, Xin M, Guo W, Hu Z, Peng H, Yao Y, Sun Q, Ni Z
evidence.evidence_sentence: Flowering time in wheat and barley is known to be modified by mutations in the Photoperiod-1 (Ppd-1) gene

edges.mechanism: Dominant Ppd-D1a shortens each internode by ~10%, reducing overall plant height.

evidence.title: The Photoperiod-Insensitive Allele Ppd-D1a Promotes Earlier Flowering in Rht12 Dwarf Plants of Bread Wheat
evidence.authors: Chen Z, Cheng X, Chai L, Wang Z, Du D, Wang Z, Bian R, Zhao A, Xin M, Guo W, Hu Z, Peng H, Yao Y, Sun Q, Ni Z
evidence.evidence_sentence: the dominant Ppd-D1a allele decreases plant height in both the dwarf and tall lines, though its effect is much weaker than that of Rht12
evidence.claim: Ppd-D1a reduces plant height via internode shortening

evidence.title: The Photoperiod-Insensitive Allele Ppd-D1a Promotes Earlier Flowering in Rht12 Dwarf Plants of Bread Wheat
evidence.authors: Chen Z, Cheng X, Chai L, Wang Z, Du D, Wang Z, Bian R, Zhao A, Xin M, Guo W, Hu Z, Peng H, Yao Y, Sun Q, Ni Z
evidence.evidence_sentence: Plant height was reduced by either Rht12 or Ppd-D1a through a reduction of the length of each internode instead of a reduction of the number of internodes

edges.mechanism: Autoactive Rht-B13b (S240F) NB-LRR upregulates PR genes including class-III peroxidases.

edges.mechanism: TaOSCA1.4 hyperosmolality-gated Ca2+ channel; RNAi reduces height 11-16% via GA-independent route.

evidence.title: Cloning a novel reduced-height (Rht) gene TaOSCA1.4 from a QTL in wheat
evidence.authors: Lv K, Song J, Zhang Q, Li M, Mu W, Ma C, Wu J, Wang X, Sun J, Cui F
evidence.evidence_sentence: the average PHs of the wild-type (WT), RNAi lines 1 (Ri-1) and 2 (Ri-2) were 94.6, 83.6 and 79.2 cm, respectively
evidence.claim: TaOSCA1.4 RNAi reduces wheat plant height

evidence.title: Cloning a novel reduced-height (Rht) gene TaOSCA1.4 from a QTL in wheat
evidence.authors: Lv K, Song J, Zhang Q, Li M, Mu W, Ma C, Wu J, Wang X, Sun J, Cui F
evidence.evidence_sentence: TaOSCA1.4 gene is not directly associated with GA, which should be a novel mechanism for a wheat Rht gene
evidence.claim: TaOSCA1.4 acts independent of GA-DELLA pathway
